title: Tundra relief and snow-edge phase
summary: Sixty-second structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-3 wave gives tundra a stronger low-relief and snow-edge identity so the inland top end feels more varied without becoming harsh.

execution_order.why_now: The inland top end is ready for a small but meaningful terrain and shelter identity gain.
A compact relief beat can make tundra feel more varied without broadening traversal harshly.
The tundra should stay gentle and readable.
A second small beat can give the inland top end stronger place memory.
Snow-edge variety is the next natural extension after the first relief beat.
The branch should feel richer without harsher traversal.

guardrails: Avoid making tundra harsh, maze-like, or overly sparse.
Reuse thaw, snow-edge, and low-growth language instead of inventing another cue layer.
acceptance_focus: Tundra has a clearer low-relief identity.
The inland top end feels more varied and memorable.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-tundra-relief-and-snow-edge-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 3's next long-push follow-on after the treeline crest work: give tundra a stronger relief and snow-edge identity without turning the inland top end into a harsher traversal space.
